text.value: Choose your race. Racial ability modifiers will preview below.
If your race has a floating +2, set it in the Manual overrides on the Ability scores page.
Pick your starting class. You can add more classes here later for multiclassing.

components.value: Please pick at least one class
You have the same class listed twice. Remove the duplicate.

text.value: Multiclassing tip: BAB sums across classes (rounded down per class), and you take the best save category from each class.
Level 1 is always max hit die (PF1e rule). Beyond level 1: Max = full die, Average = half die + 1, Roll = rolled at level-up (shows the average until you roll).
Choose a generation method, then set your six ability scores (before racial modifiers).
Point buy costs (per ability): 7=-4, 8=-2, 9=-1, 10=0, 11=+1, 12=+2, 13=+3, 14=+5, 15=+7, 16=+10, 17=+13, 18=+17. Spend up to your budget; legal base range is 7-18.
Tap Roll to generate six scores with the chosen method. You can then edit the boxes to assign each roll to the ability you want.
Custom: enter any values you and your GM agree on. No budget is enforced.
Over budget — reduce one or more scores.
If your race grants a floating +2 (e.g. Human, Half-Elf, Half-Orc), bake it into the base score for the chosen ability.
Allocate your starting skill ranks. Class skills get +3 once at least 1 rank is invested. Max ranks in any skill = your character level.

components.value: Max ranks per skill at this level:

text.value: Every character gets 1 feat at 1st level (and 1 more every odd level after).
Human bonus: +1 extra feat at 1st level.
Fighter bonus: +1 bonus combat feat at 1st level (and at every even level).
Roll your starting wealth and outfit your character.
Roll the dice expression above and enter the resulting GP in the Gold field.
Pick 2 traits. By convention they must come from 2 different categories.

components.value: Both traits are in the same category. Pick a trait from a different category.

text.value: Trait bonuses are shown in full on your character sheet under Features & Feats; apply the listed bonus to the relevant roll yourself (most are a +1 or +2 trait bonus to a specific skill, save, or initiative).
Final touches: name, alignment, deity, and physical description.

components.value: Please provide a name for your character.
